name: Generate Declaration Files for TypeScript Libraries
description: Configure TypeScript to generate .d.ts declaration files and declaration maps for publishable libraries with proper package.json exports.
prerequisites: TypeScript project structured as a library

steps.title: Configure tsconfig for declaration output
steps.description: Enable declaration and declarationMap in compilerOptions.
steps.command:
cat > tsconfig.build.json << 'EOF'
{
  "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "declarationDir": "./dist/types",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "noEmit": false,
    "emitDeclarationOnly": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "exclude": ["src/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/*.spec.ts"]
}
EOF
steps.tip: Use emitDeclarationOnly: true when a bundler handles the JavaScript output. TypeScript only generates .d.ts files.

steps.title: Generate declaration files
steps.description: Run the TypeScript compiler with the build config to produce .d.ts files.

steps.title: Configure package.json exports and types
steps.description: Set the types, main, and exports fields so consumers find your type declarations.
steps.command: npm pkg set types='./dist/types/index.d.ts' main='./dist/index.js' module='./dist/index.mjs'
steps.tip: Use the exports field for conditional exports: types for .d.ts, import for ESM, require for CJS.

steps.title: Verify declarations are correct
steps.description: Check that the generated .d.ts files export the expected types.

steps.title: Test the package locally before publishing
steps.description: Use npm pack to create a tarball and install it in a test project.
steps.command: npm pack --dry-run && npm pack && echo 'Install in another project: npm install ./your-package-1.0.0.tgz'

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Generate TypeScript Declaration Files
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Generating declarations ==="
npx tsc --project tsconfig.build.json

echo ""
echo "=== Generated files ==="
find dist/types -name '*.d.ts' | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Package.json exports example ==="
cat << 'EOF'
{
  "name": "my-library",
  "types": "./dist/types/index.d.ts",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/types/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist"]
}
EOF

echo ""
echo "=== Verify with npm pack ==="
npm pack --dry-run

faq.question: What is the difference between declaration and declarationMap?
faq.answer: declaration: true generates .d.ts type definition files. declarationMap: true generates .d.ts.map files that map declarations back to source .ts files, enabling Go to Definition to jump to the original TypeScript source instead of the .d.ts file.

faq.question: Should I commit generated .d.ts files to git?
faq.answer: No. Add dist/ to .gitignore and generate declarations during your build/publish pipeline. Committing generated files creates merge conflicts and stale types.

faq.question: Why use emitDeclarationOnly instead of regular emit?
faq.answer: When using a bundler like tsup, esbuild, or Rollup for JavaScript output, you only need TypeScript to generate .d.ts files. The bundler produces faster, smaller JavaScript than tsc.
